name: workflow-plugin-digitalocean
description: DigitalOcean IaC provider: App Platform, DOKS, databases, Redis cache, load balancers, VPC, firewall, DNS, Spaces, DOCR, certificates, Droplets, Block Storage Volumes, IAM, and API gateway
author: GoCodeAlone

expose.description: Whether the service has a public edge route (public, default) or is reachable only from sibling components via DO App Platform internal DNS (<name>.internal:<port>) (internal).

infra.app_domain.description: DigitalOcean App Platform domain binding. Updates only AppSpec.Domains on an existing App Platform app so DNS/domain aliases can be reconciled without rebuilding service images or runtime environment variables.

app.description: Existing App Platform app name. Required unless app_id is set.

app_id.description: Existing App Platform app UUID. Required unless app is set.

domain.description: Fully qualified domain name to attach to the app.

type.description: App Platform custom domain type. Use PRIMARY for the app's primary custom domain or ALIAS for a non-primary custom domain.

zone.description: DigitalOcean DNS zone for provider-managed DNS validation.

wildcard.description: Whether the domain is a wildcard binding.

certificate.description: Certificate ID to attach when using a custom certificate.

minimum_tls_version.description: Minimum TLS version for this domain.

infra.dns.description: DigitalOcean DNS zone and record reconciliation. Declared records are upserted; absent_records deletes targeted stale records without making the whole zone authoritative.

records.description: Records to create or update. Each record supports type, name, data, ttl, and type-specific fields.

absent_records.description: Records to delete when present. Each entry supports type, name, and optional data for exact-value matching.

infra.firewall.description: DigitalOcean cloud firewall. Attaches to Droplets by ID or by tag (which auto-attaches future Droplets / DOKS pools that receive the tag). Either `droplet_ids` or `tags` is REQUIRED; `wfctl infra apply` rejects firewalls with no targets before any DO API call. NOTE: DO firewalls do not attach to App Platform apps — for App-Platform-only deployments, use `expose: internal` services plus `trusted_sources` on managed databases.

droplet_ids.description: Droplet IDs the firewall attaches to. At least one of `droplet_ids` or `tags` must be set.

tags.description: Droplet/DOKS-pool tag strings. Resources receiving any listed tag auto-join the firewall. Example: ["bmw-prod"].

examples.name: tag-based-firewall
examples.comment: Tag-based attachment so future Droplets / DOKS pools auto-join.
